name: wallets
context: Wallet creation, key management, and transaction signing for Ethereum and EVM chains
info: Wallets hold private keys and sign transactions. For agent workflows, wallets are typically derived from a private key hex string or mnemonic phrase. ethers.js Wallet and viem's privateKeyToAccount are the standard programmatic interfaces. For production, use hardware wallets (Ledger), cloud KMS (AWS KMS, GCP KMS), or MPC wallets (Coinbase Wallet SDK, Fireblocks). Never store raw private keys in source code — use environment variables or secret managers. Wallets need ETH for gas (except for EIP-3009 x402 payments) and the relevant tokens for the operation.

sections.title: Creating wallets
sections.body: Use `new Wallet(privateKey, provider)` in ethers.js or `privateKeyToAccount(key)` + `createWalletClient()` in viem. Generate random wallets with `Wallet.createRandom()`. Derive from mnemonic with `Wallet.fromPhrase()`.

sections.title: Key storage options
sections.body: Environment variables for dev. AWS/GCP KMS for production. Hardware wallets for high-value. MPC wallets for institutional. Never commit keys to source. Always use `.gitignore` for `.env` files.

sections.title: Transaction signing
sections.body: Wallets sign transactions locally, then submit to the network. `wallet.sendTransaction()` (ethers) or `client.sendTransaction()` (viem) handle signing and submission. For [[ethereum/x402]], the SDK signs EIP-3009 authorizations (see [[ethereum/x402#payment-schemes]]), not raw transactions.

sections.title: Funding requirements
sections.body: ETH for gas on all operations except EIP-3009 [[ethereum/x402]] payments (facilitator pays gas — see [[ethereum/x402#facilitator]]). USDC for x402 payments. Relevant tokens for transfers. Use testnet faucets for development. See [[ethereum/gas]] for current fee estimates.
